negativeExample: T84 reads earnedPoints from event payload, validates against formula
positiveExample: T84 reads answers[] and timeTaken only. earnedPoints field in input has no downstream use — not read, not validated, not referenced.
teachingPoint: Any score or metric affecting user reputation, leaderboard position, or earned rewards must be calculated server-side from raw inputs. Client-submitted metric values have no legitimate use and must never be read. Anti-farming is a MACHINE trust boundary, not a validation: the input field simply has no downstream use.
discriminatingConstraint: T84 input shape = answers[] + timeTaken. earnedPoints absent. There is no code path where a client-submitted point value influences the total.
keywords: server side calculation points anti-farming trust boundary earned points client payload

negativeExample: Server computes streak boundary from UTC date — same day = no streak
positiveExample: T96 receives userTimezoneOffset. 'Today' = UTC timestamp + offset → local date. Streak boundary is midnight in the user's local timezone.
teachingPoint: When a feature depends on 'today' from the user's perspective, the server must compute local date from UTC + offset. UTC-only comparison silently breaks streaks for users in positive-offset timezones. Store UTC. Compute local boundaries on read.
discriminatingConstraint: userTimezoneOffset is a REQUIRED input to T96. Streak boundary comparison is localDate (UTC + offset), never raw UTC date.
keywords: timezone streak UTC offset local date user perspective boundary

negativeExample: All three branches start from QuestionnaireAnswered. T90 checks privacy and blocks T91 if private mode is active.
positiveExample: T90 is the SOLE entry to the social branch. T90 emits SocialShareApproved (pass) or SocialShareSkipped (skip). T91-T93 trigger on SocialShareApproved — never on QuestionnaireAnswered.
teachingPoint: An optional branch must be gated at its entry point. The gate node emits the entry event for its branch. Downstream nodes trigger on that entry event — never on the original flow trigger. If two branches share a trigger, a gate on one cannot prevent the other from starting.
discriminatingConstraint: T91 @EventPattern = SocialShareApproved (T90's output). T91 does not trigger on QuestionnaireAnswered. There is no code path where T91 starts before T90's privacy check completes.
keywords: privacy gate branch sole entry social share approved event pattern topology

negativeExample: T87 evaluates criteria against the current submission only
positiveExample: T87 reads xiigen-achievement-records before any emit. If achievementId already exists for (userId, achievementId): idempotent return. No second emit. Only NEW achievements produce AchievementUnlocked.
teachingPoint: Achievement unlocks are idempotent by (userId, achievementId). A second qualifying submission must not emit a second AchievementUnlocked. History validation is required before any unlock emit.
discriminatingConstraint: T87 reads xiigen-achievement-records before emit. If record exists: return success without emitting. There is no code path that emits AchievementUnlocked without first checking achievement history.
keywords: achievement idempotency history unlock double emit prevention userId achievementId

negativeExample: Apply all ML suggestions directly — the model is trusted
positiveExample: Three guards before applying: (1) count ceiling ≤3, (2) protected invariants: drop required module changes, (3) recency guard: <2 lessons → LearningPlanSkipped (success)
teachingPoint: ML model outputs must be validated before application against: (1) a count ceiling, (2) protected invariants (required modules cannot be removed), (3) recency guards. Apply what passes all three. ML models are not constrained by business rules.
discriminatingConstraint: T89 applies changes.slice(0, maxChanges) where maxChanges ≤ 3. Required modules absent from applied changes. Recency check runs before ML call. LearningPlanSkipped is a success — not a failure or an error.
keywords: ML output validation guards count ceiling required modules recency LearningPlanSkipped

negativeExample: Wait for all three branches (A, B, C) before emitting LearningFlowCompleted
positiveExample: LearningFlowCompleted gates on Branch A completion only (T85 committed, T96 done, T87 done). Branch B emits LearningPlanAdapted independently. Branch C emits QuestionnairePostDistributed independently.
teachingPoint: When parallel branches have different time scales, the completion event gates on the fastest branch that satisfies the user's SLA. Slower branches emit their own events independently. Coupling all branches in one gate multiplies the latency.
discriminatingConstraint: LearningFlowCompleted emitted when T85+T96+T87 complete. LearningPlanAdapted and QuestionnairePostDistributed are NOT in the gate condition. There is no code path where LearningFlowCompleted waits for Branch B or C.
keywords: parallel branches time decoupled completion gate SLA Branch A LearningFlowCompleted
